mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate profit margins and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1 deceleration and CPIAUCSL acceleration yields positive 60d returns in > 55% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels indicate robust manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO against 5d forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4 percent, the cost of capital for leveraged buyouts and corporate debt refinancing rises sharply, forcing equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns remain positive in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in real personal spending reflects immediate consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability yields negative 60d returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate strong residential investment demand and construction sector activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for HOUST against 20d returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
